Project Title: Evaluation of existing software for detecting AI generated multimedia files

Project Title: Deepfakes: The impacts, ethical challenges and countermeasures
Project Description: Identify the impact of deepfakes from a economical, social, political perspective. What applications are used and what tools are in place to prevent and detect them.

Project Title: Email spoofing: Weaknesses and how to encounter them
Project Description: Identify how is it nowadays to create spoofing emails, used to threaten or blackmail people for financial gains. What are the weaknesses, what tools are used by email providers and how we could use technologies such as AI to prevent them.

Project Title: Runtime Detection of Data Leakage in Large Language Models
Project Description: This project focuses on designing a lightweight runtime monitoring mechanism that detects potential data leakage during LLM interactions. Students will analyse prompts and outputs to identify signs of sensitive data exposure (e.g., PII, confidential strings, training data memorisation). The system can use rule-based checks, entropy analysis, or simple ML classifiers to flag risky responses in real time. Evaluation can be performed using synthetic sensitive prompts and open-source LLMs.

Project Title: Detecting Adversarial Prompt Attacks Using Prompt Pattern Analysis
Project Description: This project investigates common adversarial prompt techniques such as prompt injection, jailbreaking, and role manipulation. Students will build a detection model that analyses prompt structure, intent shifts, and linguistic patterns to classify prompts as benign or adversarial. The system may combine heuristics (e.g., instruction override signals) with supervised learning using curated adversarial prompt datasets.

Project Title: Security Risk Assessment of Agentic AI Systems
Project Description: This project examines the security risks introduced by autonomous, tool-using AI agents (e.g., task agents with memory, API access, or file execution). Students will model threat scenarios such as excessive autonomy, unintended actions, or privilege escalation. The outcome can be a risk assessment framework or a proof-of-concept sandbox that enforces constraints like action whitelisting, permission checks, and execution boundaries.

Creating an AI-powered browser extension that detects phishing websites by analyzing URL patterns, page content, and comparing with a database of known phishing sites. The system can alert users when they are about to enter a phishing site. Key components/services: Real-time detection, warning notifications, and a simple dashboard for users to report phishing sites.

Project Title:
Designing a Penetration Testing Methodology for Secure Code Review in 
DevOps Pipelines
Project Description: Develop a methodology for integrating penetration testing into DevOps pipelines, focusing on secure code review. The project will involve creating guidelines for automated security scans, manual code reviews, and testing application security throughout the CI/CD process to identify vulnerabilities early in the development cycle

Project Title:
Evaluating Adversarial Machine Learning Attacks on Network-Based 
Intrusion Detection Systems
Project Description: Conduct a study to evaluate the impact of adversarial machine learning attacks, such as evasion and poisoning, on network-based intrusion detection systems (NIDS). Propose and test defensive strategies to enhance NIDS robustness against such adversarial techniques.

Project Title:
Development of Ransomware Pre-encryption Detection and Recovery 
Tool for Android Systems
Project Description:
Design and develop a tool that detects ransomware activities on Android OS by monitoring memory and file system behaviors 
and provides automated recovery solutions through memory forensics methods.

Memory-Based Metamorphic Malware Forensics is a final-year project that investigates techniques for detecting and analyzing advanced malware that continually changes its structure and primarily resides in system memory. The project focuses on memory forensics and behavioral analysis to identify malicious activity that bypasses traditional static and signature-based detection methods. It aims to extract forensic artifacts from volatile memory to support malware detection, understanding, and attribution.

Project Title:
Real-time Intrusion Detection System Using SIEM Integration and 
Threat Intelligence Feeds
Project Description: Create a real-time intrusion detection system (IDS) that integrates with Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) systems and utilizes threat intelligence feeds to enhance detection capabilities against emerging threats in network environments.

Project Title:
Implementation of Blockchain for Secure Chain-of-Custody in Digital 
Forensic Investigations
Project Description: Investigate the application of blockchain technology to ensure the secure chain-of-custody of digital evidence in forensic investigations. The project will involve developing a prototype blockchain system to log forensic data transactions, making them tamper-proof and auditable.

Have you ever used your google account to sign into an app not developed by google? Federated Identity Management enables users registered with the Identity Provider within one domain to access services at a partner domain, without requiring them to also register with the identity provider in the second domain. Replacing bilateral agreements between identifiers with a network thereof provides scope to enable a large, scalable, decentralised network of users and services. This project aims to resolve the challenge of routing authentication requests and responses around such a decentralised network of identity providers.

What's your favourite peer-to-peer network? Distributed Hash Tables (DHT) are decentralised and distributed means of providing a lookup service that underlie peer-to-peer networking. However, DHT-based systems are prone to security attacks, e.g., Sybil and Eclipse attacks. Existing surveys of the security issues and proposed solutions do not cover the full range of DHT algorithms nor vulnerabilities. This project shall aim to contribute to the existing literature by identifying and addressing gaps in the security analysis of DHTs

Do you want to join a revolution in software engineering enabling users to experience the final system before a single line of code of its implementation has been written? This project shall provide a realistic visualisation front-end to enable end users to interact with a mathematical model of a software architecture/design as if it were the final implementation. To create an interactive visualisation of the Event-B models, you will be using BMotionWeb, a tool built on top of the ProB Java API, although no prior knowledge of these technologies is necessary for the project to be a success.

Deep learning (DL) has transformed image recognition and analysis by enabling unprecedented performance improvements in computer vision tasks in recent years. Project Title: Use ML methods to explore wound size measurement
Project Description: The student needs to source a dataset with images of wounds ideally from diabetes foot ulcers. Then explore at least 3 available algorithms related to wound size measurement and tune the hyperparameters to achieve the best accuracy.

Project Title: Evaluation of mHealth Applications for heart failure, diabetes or other long term conditions
Project Description: The student is called to evaluate using mixed methods the acceptability and usability of the mHealth applications. Potential methods may include (heuristics, SUS, TAM) potential outcomes could be a dashboard or framework.

AI healthcare mobile applications are gaining popularity. The student is called to evaluate the usability of the AI powered mHealth applications. Potential methods may include (heuristics, SUS, ISO 9241-11) potential outcomes could be a dashboard or framework.

Synthetic data generation methods for business purposes including health. We currently generate synthetic datasets instead of synthetic data for use cases. The student need to use workflows, AI and logic to mitigate the noise, tailor this and generate less breadth and more in depth data.

You will use network simulation to assess how a 6G-based telemedicine system performs under varying loads, latencies, and mobility conditions. The goal is to measure end-to-end delay, reliability, and throughput when transmitting medical data such as video, sensor streams, and control signals between patients, edge nodes, and hospitals. You will compare baseline and adaptive network configurations to determine how effectively 6G features such as network slicing and edge computing, can sustain the low-latency, high-reliability requirements of remote healthcare applications.

Project Title: Federated learning in electronic health record data
Project Description: Build a model that trains across multiple “virtual NHS hospitals” without sharing raw data, using federated learning to combine model updates.

Create a lightweight end-to-end pipeline that simulates 3-4 hospitals: ETL synthetic/opensource EHR into FHIR tables, harmonise features, then run FL with secure aggregation and optional differential privacy.

Project Title: Document verification and summaries for eligibility verification
Project Description:
Project client: Citizens Advice, a charity that supports people facing real challenges, such as rising energy costs, debt, and housing issues. Through this project, you’ll work with mock documents simulating real data, gain practical experience, and make a meaningful impact on people’s lives.
Citizens Advice advisers manually review client submitted documents—such as bank statements or energy bills—to verify identity or eligibility for support, which is time consuming. This project aims to use AI to summarise documents, extract key details (e.g., name, address, account number), and flag inconsistencies for further review. It will likely involve OCR, Natural Language Processing, and Large Language Models, with a strong focus on securely handling sensitive data.

Project Title: Predicting debt problems for different demographic groups
Project Description:
Project client: Citizens Advice, a charity that supports people facing real challenges, such as rising energy costs, debt, and housing issues. Through this project, you’ll work with real data, gain practical experience, and make a meaningful impact on people’s lives.
This project will examine whether demographic and household factors, such as age, income, household type, and housing tenure, can predict who is most likely to face future debt problems. Using anonymised client?level or aggregated data, you will build and evaluate predictive models to identify at?risk groups. The goal is to generate insights that support earlier, more targeted interventions, helping Citizens Advice allocate resources effectively and prevent debt issues from escalating.

Project Title: Identifying distinct client groups and their needs
Project Description:
Project client: Citizens Advice, a charity that supports people facing real challenges, such as rising energy costs, debt, and housing issues. Through this project, you’ll work with real data, gain practical experience, and make a meaningful impact on people’s lives.
This project will use cluster analysis to identify groups of clients who share similar characteristics, circumstances, or advice?seeking behaviours. By analysing demographic profiles, presenting issues, service?use patterns, and outcomes, you will uncover distinct client segments and describe their needs, barriers, and support preferences. The goal is to develop a segmentation framework that helps tailor interventions, improve client journeys, allocate resources more effectively, and design outreach that better meets the needs of different groups.

Project Title: Impact of weather conditions on service demand
Project Description:
Project client: Citizens Advice, a charity that supports people facing real challenges, such as rising energy costs, debt, and housing issues. Through this project, you’ll work with real data, gain practical experience, and make a meaningful impact on people’s lives.
This project will examine how weather conditions, such as temperature, heavy rainfall, cold snaps, or heatwaves, affect demand for Citizens Advice services. By linking historical service?use data with weather trends, you will identify patterns, model demand changes, and highlight which advice areas are most affected. The goal is to help Citizens Advice anticipate demand and plan resources more effectively during weather?related surges.

The objective is to use of Semantic Video Understanding through Text Video in different applications including:
Captioning: Automatically generating captions for videos to make content accessible to people with disabilities or for use in media indexing.
Video Summarization: Creating text summaries for quick consumption of video content (Generating movie trailers)
Semantic Video Retrieval: Using textual descriptions to search and retrieve specific video segments from large datasets.
Content Moderation: Automatically generating descriptions of video content for detecting inappropriate or harmful content.
Analyze malware behaviour, Malware family classification based on behavioral explanation

Project Title: Quantum Fuzzy Machine Learning for Cybersecurity Threat Detection
Project Description: This project investigates the integration of fuzzy logic with hybrid quantum-classical machine learning for cybersecurity threat detection. The proposed framework will combine fuzzy inference systems with variational quantum circuits to improve uncertainty handling and adaptive anomaly detection in dynamic cyber environments.

Project Title: Quantum Federated Learning for Secure Healthcare Systems
Project Description: This project explores hybrid Quantum Federated Learning approaches for privacy-preserving healthcare analytics. The framework will integrate federated learning with quantum-enhanced optimisation and classification techniques to enable secure distributed learning across healthcare institutions without sharing sensitive patient data.

Project Title: Quantum Reinforcement Learning for Financial Fraud Detection
Project Description: This project investigates the use of Quantum Reinforcement Learning (QRL) for adaptive fraud detection in financial systems. The study will explore how quantum-enhanced decision-making and sequential learning can improve the detection of evolving fraudulent transaction patterns in real time.

Project Title: Explainable Quantum Machine Learning for Intelligent IoT Security
Project Description: This project focuses on developing Explainable Quantum Machine Learning (XQML) models for IoT security applications. It aims to integrate explainable AI techniques with hybrid quantum-classical learning models to improve transparency, interpretability, and trust in cyber-attack detection systems.

Project Title: Quantum Swarm Intelligence for Smart City Optimisation
Project Description: This project explores the combination of quantum-inspired swarm intelligence techniques with quantum machine learning for smart city optimisation problems such as traffic management, energy allocation, and urban resource planning. The study will investigate adaptive optimisation strategies for complex large-scale environments.

Investigate multi-modal Natural User Interfaces (NUI). Investigate the opportunities and implications of combining NUI input methods. For example, develop a voice-based command system with object of intent given by eye-tracker. The idea would be that the voice-based commands could be made simpler by using eye-tracking to give the object for the command. Voice and eye-tracker input could be implemented or simulated.

An example would be context aware adaptation based upon voice or gaze input e.g. the interface would adapt to the current focus point or voice command. The voice and gaze input could either be implemented or simulated.

You will use network simulation to assess how a 6G-based telemedicine system performs under varying loads, latencies, and mobility conditions. The goal is to measure end-to-end delay, reliability, and throughput when transmitting medical data such as video, sensor streams, and control signals between patients, edge nodes, and hospitals. Project Title: What is web credibility in the age of AI?
Project Description: In this research project, you will compile the research on web credibility and trust, and identify the key components of web design that influence credibility. You will then create several versions of a mock website, varying the design based on the degree to which AI has been used to support the design - this may include elements such as AI-generated images and text. You will then conduct a mixed methos study using the designs and statistically analyse how AI-use visibility impacts whether a user trusts the design.

Project Title: Exploring motivation and dis-engagement with healthy living applications.
Project Description: In this project you will investigate why health behaviour change interventions fail and design a solution (a fragment of an application - this could be a notification schedule, persuasive messages, or gamification elements) that could be added to an application to maintain engagement. You will then experimentally compare 2 designs to evaluate which is more likely to increase motivation.

Project Title: Embracing the Slab of Distraction in the classroom - how can we use mobiles in the classroom productively?
Project Description: Mobile phones are considered by schools to be a nuisance and distraction to learning. In this project, you will flip this idea on its head and consider how they could be used productively as split-screening learning aids during class. You will research academic literature on learning and novel approaches to using digital devices in learning spaces, and propose a set of guidelines on design of apps that prevent distraction that could be used in a classroom setting. You will prototype an example of an app that meets these guidelines and qualitatively evaluate it.

Project Title: An application to support blended environment tabletop gaming.
Project Description: There are applications that support online gameplay of tabletop games, and others that support groups of people in a room together - but few support blended gameplay. Develop an application that will support people to play tabletop RPGs (such as D&D) with paper and pencil and real dice with friends remotely.

Project Title: User-centred design of applications that address breaking procrastination cycles
Project Description: Research, develop and evaluate a short training app that helps people understand why they procrastinate and break their procrastination cycles through understanding the cognitive mechanisms behind it.

Project Title: Perfection or Authenticity - how has AI impacted our perspective on good design?
Project Description: Professionalism has long been held as a marker for good UX and trust: however, AI is now able to create highly professional digital content. What is the difference between human-made design and AI-gen design in web apps/computer games and how does that impact our appraisal of them? In this project you will compare AI-gen and human-made designs in a field (this could be digital art, games, or web apps) and conduct research with humans - collect qualitative data on their thoughts on these designs, and quantitative evaluations on them.

Project Title: How do AI tools impact learner wellbeing?
Project Description: LLM use has rapidly expanded into universities. But what impact has it had on how students feel about their education? And how can we support/mitigate the impact that is has on our wellbeing? Conduct qualitative research into LLM use and weelbeing in students, and propose a solution e.g. training or adjustments to LLMs to mitigate any negative effects.

Project Title: AI v Dev - Comparing a LLM-generated web app with a traditionally built app
Project Description: The aim of this case study-based engineering/research project is to pick a web app, and build it twice - once using AI, and once without AI. You will then compare the applications in terms of metrics such as usability, build time, speed, maintainability, scalability, and evaluate which approach is best with consideration to these metrics.
Machine learning based recommendation system (engineering)

Project Title: Fake News Detection on Social Media Using Hybrid ML–LLM Approaches
Project Description: This project explores automated detection of fake or misleading information on social media by combining traditional machine learning approaches with large language models (LLMs). Students will compare classical text-based classifiers with LLM-based zero-shot or few-shot methods, analysing performance, robustness, and explainability.

Project Title: Automatic Summarisation of Social Media Discussions
Project Description: This project aims to develop systems that automatically summarise large volumes of social media comments or discussions into concise and meaningful summaries. Students will experiment with extractive and abstractive summarisation techniques, including LLM-based approaches, and evaluate summary quality using both automatic metrics and qualitative analysis.

Project Title: Predicting User Engagement on Social Media Using ML Models
Project Description: This project investigates how machine learning models can be used to predict user engagement on social media platforms, such as likes, shares, and comments. Students will explore feature engineering techniques using content, temporal and user-level attributes, and compare the performance of different supervised learning models. The project focuses on data-driven modelling and evaluation of predictive performance.

Project Title: Using LLMs for Content Moderation in Social Media
Project Description: This project examines the use of large language models for automated content moderation tasks, such as detecting hate speech, abusive language, or policy-violating content. Students will design and evaluate moderation pipelines using prompt-based LLMs and compare them with traditional classification models.

This project focuses on identifying abnormal or suspicious behaviour in social networks, such as bot activity, spam campaigns or unusual interaction patterns. Using unsupervised or semi-supervised machine learning methods, students will analyse user behaviour and network features to detect anomalies.

The human aspects of an organisation can be both the strongest and weakest element, largely depending on how you handle your staff and get them involved in your organisation. This study would look at how a particular organisation makes use of it's personnel as a security resources (training, motivation, education)

Digital games are massively popular, but generally not considered a serious security concern. You would examine how video games are abused by cybercriminals. This could be anything from using hacked games to install malware, the creation of games to distribute illegal content, how mods are made/used within games to scams conducted through gaming communication channels.

While substantial research is being conducted into the technical use of AI within cybersecurity, less is being down with reviewing how AI is being incorperated in a more general sense into organisations. You could explore the security implications of employees uploading content to GenAI models, the reliabilty of cyber security advice provided by AI, how AI can be used in social-engineering attacks or anything else that examines the security issues of GenAI usage.

Block-based programming languages such as Scratch (scratch.mit.edu) have made it easier for children to learn the basics of programming. For example, syntax errors are avoided because code blocks can only be joined together when syntactically legal. We have designed and prototyped PyBlocks - a blocks-based environment for programming directly in Python. PyBlocks aims to provide some of the advantages of systems like Scratch to school and university students learning programming in Python. The aim of this project is to develop an extension to the current PyBlocks, or a variation for a different language. This project would require strong JavaScript skills.

The aim of this project is to implement the design ideas presented in the paper "A Block Design for Introductory Functional Programming in Haskell" (Poole, 2019) that provide the basis for a block-based environment for novice programmers to learn functional programming in Haskell. This project would require strong JavaScript skills

Project Title: Eye-tracking and data collection from novice programmers
Project Description: Do you know your gherkin from your cucumber? This project aims to unite Formal Modelling with Behaviour Driven Development. Business stakeholders write acceptance tests in collaboration with the development team to capture the desired behaviour of the system. These can be understood at both a technical and non-technical level, thus act as both system requirements and acceptance criteria. When formal modelling is also required within a rigorous software engineering development process and alongside this there is a desire to use behaviour driven development then there may be duplication of effort or conflicts between the approaches. This project shall identify and demonstrate circumstances in which the two approaches can be united; you will be writing gherkin specifications and Event-B models.
Using/adapting Google Blockly to edit programs directly in a real programming language.

develop a prototype system that consist of a wireless camera for capturing images or videos and a small computer (like Raspberry Pi with wireless connectivity) to process the imaging data. The objective would be to create a sensor that can capture images and do some AI processing inside the sensor (i.e. at the edge). You can select a specific application with existing software packages available (so you do not need to develop it from scratch), for instance counting people, or detecting if someone is lying on the floor (a fall), etc. The aim would be to demonstrate that the sensor can do analysis at the edge and send out only the information of interest, rather than raw images.

the aim of this project is to implement a system for monitoring wellbeing of patients in hospital wards using computer vision and AI techniques. The project would be useful, for example, to support nurses in hospitals with looking after patients. Possible scenarios can include monitoring patient position, falls, food or water intake, and other wellbeing and safety aspects.

the aim of this project is to implement a system for monitoring people taking their medication correctly (e.g. at the right time, the right medicines, etc) from video using computer vision and artificial intelligence techniques. The project would be useful, for example, for helping elderly people living on their own to take their meds correctly and providing piece of mind to their family.

the aim of this project is to implement a system for monitoring personal hygiene practices, such as handwashing, to ensure that people adhere to the recommended practises (e.g. washing with soap for at least 20 sec). The system can use video input, computer vision and artificial intelligence techniques. The project could be useful for monitoring hygiene in hospitals and other settings.

Project Title: Monitoring social distancing and infection protection measures:
Project Description: the aim of this project is to implement a system for detecting situations where people may not be adhering to social distancing rules or not following protection measures (e.g. face coverings, etc) from video using computer vision and artificial intelligence techniques. The project would be useful for monitoring risks levels for infection control such as COVID in various environments.

the aim of this project is to implement a system for recognising typical activities of daily living in video using computer vision and artificial intelligence techniques. The application of this system would in Ambient Assisted Living (AAL), which is concerned with using various technological solutions to allow people with additional care needs live independently in their preferred environment. AAL is of great importance for future healthcare services.

Project Title: Recognising daily activities with multi-modal sensors:

The project can investigate how different types of sensors (e.g. video, sound, motion, etc) can be combined to improve performance. The application of this system would in Ambient Assisted Living (AAL), which is concerned with using various technological solutions to allow people with additional care needs live independently in their preferred environment. AAL is of great importance for future healthcare services.

the aim of this project is to implement a system for recognising domestic emergency or dangerous situations in video (such as falls, leaving switched on cooker or running water) using computer vision and artificial intelligence techniques. A practical application of AI in health technoglogies, particularly in assisted living and wellbeing

develop an environmental sensor that can be accessed remotely via the Internet. At the minimum, the sensor would need to monitor temperature. Other parameters, such as relative humidity can also be added. The sensor would need to log the data and provide access via the Internet (e.g. using a web browser). The project will need to investigate suitable hardware for the sensor (for instance a microcontroller or a Raspberry Pi) and develop software to provide data logging and networked access.

Project Title: Acceptability and adoption of health technologies
Project Description: The aim of the project is to study acceptability issues and possible barriers for adoption of emerging health and wellbeing technologies.  As health and wellbeing technologies are becoming more advanced, they use more sensors, collect more data, get involved in more aspects of people's lives. This presents new concerns for users related to privacy, security, and usability. There are also multiple stakeholders being involved, such as family members, carers, medical practitioners. Thus, there is a need to gain better understanding of these issues and ideally inform the design and development of future health and wellbeing systems and products.

Project Title: Enhancing Collision Avoidance in Industrial Robot Manipulators through Gaussian Mixture Model-Based Potential Fields
Project Description: This topic could focus on the development and implementation of an obstacle avoidance algorithm that combines Gaussian mixture models with potential fields (or other approaches) to improve collision avoidance capabilities in industrial robot manipulators. The research could delve into the technical details of the algorithm, its effectiveness, and real-world applications. The research could address issues related to dynamic obstacles, scalability, and the algorithm's ability to adapt to changing environments, making it suitable for industrial automation applications.

Project Title: Improving Task Efficiency Through Enhanced Human-Robot Collaboration.
Project Description: The demand for human-robot collaboration is increasing across sectors such as manufacturing, healthcare, and logistics, highlighting the growing need for ongoing enhancements in collaborative methods between humans and robots in these environments. This project proposes to develop and optimise human-robot collaboration algorithms within various settings/industries, with the aim of enhancing task efficiency and overall productivity.

Project Title: Robot Adaptability through Observational Learning: A Study in Human-Robot Interaction
Project Description: The project centers on the development of adaptive learning methods that enable robots to observe and learn from human behaviours. By mimicking human actions and responses, these robots can adapt to new tasks or changes in environmental conditions, enhancing their versatility and usefulness in various applications. The adaptive learning method involves the use of machine learning techniques to facilitate the robots' learning process.

Project Title: Accurate Real-Time Object Dimension Estimation Using Camera Calibration and Computer Vision
Project Description: This project addresses the problem of deriving reliable real-world object measurements from 2D camera images by proposing a Computer Vision–based approach that uses a known planar reference (A4 paper) to establish metric scale. By leveraging geometric transformations and image processing techniques, the project aims to reduce measurement uncertainty and improve accuracy without requiring specialised hardware such as depth sensors or stereo camera systems.

Project Title: Machine Learning–Driven Waste Classification Using Computer Vision and Interactive GUI
Project Description: This project addresses the problem of automating waste classification using low-cost Computer Vision and machine learning techniques. The proposed system focuses on classifying waste items into four predefined categories, covering eight distinct waste objects, using image-based recognition. By leveraging supervised learning and visual features, the system aims to demonstrate how common waste items can be reliably identified and categorised using a standard camera and trained classification model.
